item.title: EU Chips Act Revamp Would Let Commission Invest Directly in Fabs
item.description: The European Union’s revamped plans to stimulate the semiconductor industry would allow the bloc’s executive arm to invest directly in manufacturing, people familiar with the draft said.

item.title: US Jobless Claims Plunge to 189,000, Lowest Since 1969
item.description: Applications for US unemployment benefits plunged to the lowest level in decades, a sign that job-cut announcements have not yet meaningfully translated into layoffs.

item.title: US GDP Rose 2% in Early 2026 in Sign of Economy’s Resilience
item.description: US economic growth accelerated at the start of the year, fueled by solid business and consumer demand.

item.title: Polymarket Adds New Detection Tools After Insider Bet Backlash
item.description: Polymarket is partnering with blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis Inc. to help police its platform as prediction markets grapple with increased scrutiny over insider trading.

item.title: Canada Economy Tracking 1.7% Annualized Growth in First Quarter
item.description: The Canadian economy bounced back in the first quarter after a softer end to 2025, with growth driven by an expansion in goods-producing industries.

item.title: Mexican Economy Posts Largest Drop in a Quarter Since 2020
item.description: Mexico’s economy shrunk by the most in nearly six years in the first quarter in the latest economic setback for President Claudia Sheinbaum and despite her efforts to boost local and foreign investment to stimulate growth.

item.title: ECB Holds Rates With War’s Hit to Economy Still Unfolding
item.description: The European Central Bank kept interest rates unchanged, with officials signaling they need more time to assess the extent of the Iran war’s jolt to the economy.

item.title: UK Two-Year Bonds Extend Gains as BOE Governor Bailey Speaks
item.description: UK bonds extended a rally as traders pared bets on Bank of England interest-rate hikes, after officials left policy unchanged and Governor Andrew Bailey it was in a “reasonable place.”

item.title: US Equity Futures Rise as Mega-Cap Earnings Lift Tech, Oil Drops
item.description: US stock futures advanced on Thursday after some mega-cap and other tech companies reported bullish earnings lifting their stocks, while oil prices fell from a four-year high.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 30, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.4% as of 7:48 a.m. in New York after a blockbuster slate of technology earnings.

item.title: Emerging Stocks Set for Best Month Since 2009 Despite Oil Fear
item.description: Emerging equities were in the red on the last trading day of April after oil prices surged above $125 per barrel, though they stayed on track for their best month since 2009.

item.title: OPEC+ Delegates Expect Another Symbolic Supply Hike Without UAE
item.description: OPEC+ is likely to agree another symbolic production increase for June, in the group’s first move since the surprise departure of the United Arab Emirates, three delegates said.

item.title: Meta Looks to Raise as Much as $25 Billion With Jumbo Bond Sale
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is looking to sell between $20 billion and $25 billion of investment-grade bonds, according to people with knowledge of the transaction, as the Facebook parent boosts spending on infrastructure for the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: European Power Plunges to Record Negative Prices on Solar Surge
item.description: European power prices slid to record lows for Friday — being set at huge negative rates in Germany and France — as strong solar generation coincides with public holidays across much of the region.

item.title: Eric Trump Disappears From Troubled Fintech’s Public Leadership
item.description: Eric Trump has disappeared from the public leadership of a small Las Vegas-based company closely tied to his family’s crypto firm, World Liberty Financial Inc.

item.title: Iran Internet Blackout Tightens Military’s Grip on Civilian Life
item.description: Iran’s record internet blackout is emerging as a key dividing line between the country’s powerful security apparatus and its civilian government, underlining how the war with the US has extended the military’s control over daily life.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Key Russian Refinery, Pumping Station in Perm
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck a major refinery deep inside Russia and again attacked an oil-pumping station nearby, further crippling Moscow’s crude-processing capability.

item.title: Prada Sales Meet Views as War Slows Growth at Miu Miu Brand
item.description: Prada SpA reported first quarter sales that met expectations, weathering war in the Middle East that dragged on sales of the fashion group’s popular Miu Miu line.

item.title: Royal Caribbean Shares Jump as Outlook Suggests Resilient Demand
item.description: Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. shares rose as the company’s outlook for the year suggests it will be able to weather headwinds such as higher fuel costs and disruptions on its Middle Eastern itineraries.

item.title: Chinese Demand Destruction Is Key to Calming Global LNG Prices
item.description: Years of planning helped Beijing to cut imports, freeing up more fuel for other markets.

item.title: Warsh Faces High Hurdle for Rate Cuts as Powell Readies to Pass the Baton
item.description: Jerome Powell says the Fed leadership handover to Kevin Warsh will be “normal.” Some might disagree.

item.title: Polish Rate Cut Chances Are Quickly Evaporating, Dabrowski Says
item.description: Polish interest-rate cut chances significantly diminished after inflation accelerated in April, according to policymaker Ireneusz Dabrowski.

item.title: Ukraine Holds Key Rate as Energy Price Surge Fuels Inflation
item.description: Ukraine held interest rates for a second meeting as policymakers weigh an acceleration in inflation in an economy already battered by more than four years of Russia’s invasion.

item.title: Qualcomm Bids for a Share of the Massive Spending on AI Chips
item.description: The top maker of chips for smartphones wants in on the explosive AI data center market.

item.title: Blue Owl’s Asset Growth Meet Estimates Amid Direct Lending Slump
item.description: Blue Owl Capital Inc.’s fee-related earnings and assets increased as the alternative investment firm leaned on other parts of its business amid souring sentiment toward private credit.

item.title: It’s Boating Season, But Only If You Can Afford Fuel
item.description: In normal times, boaters spend their money at marinas, tackle shops and dockside restaurants, keeping local businesses buzzing. This year, they’re spending it all at the pump.

item.title: Bank of England Holds Rates as Officials Consider Hikes Ahead
item.description: The Bank of England kept interest rates on hold with several policymakers saying they might consider future hikes, just as oil prices soared within reach of the central bank’s most pessimistic scenario for the economy.

item.title: Hormuz Blockade Boosts Inland Container Flows at UAE Port Outside the Strait
item.description: With Hormuz closed, trucks are rerouting containers unable to reach one of the world’s busiest ports

item.title: Hedge Fund Manager ‘Ignoring Trump’ Nets 39% Gain on Energy Bets
item.description: A hedge fund manager focused on navigating the energy transition has generated a 39% return so far this year, after adopting a policy of ignoring US President Donald Trump’s signals on the Iran war.

item.title: SpaceX Investor 137 Raises $700 Million Ahead of Musk-Led IPO
item.description: 137 Ventures, a repeat investor in Elon Musk’s SpaceX, has raised $700 million across two new funds to back startups over longer time horizons.
